mission.title: Install and Configure NATS
mission.description: NATS is a high-performance messaging system designed for cloud-native applications. It is ideal for microservices architectures, IoT, and real-time data streaming. Key trade-offs include simplicity and performance versus advanced features found in more complex messaging systems.

steps.title: Add the NATS Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official NATS Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add nats https://nats-io.github.io/k8s/helm/charts

```

> This is the official NATS Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install NATS with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the NATS server using Helm in the 'nats' namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace nats

helm install nats nats/nats --namespace nats --version 2.11.0

```

> Create a namespace for NATS.
> Install NATS server with a specific version.

steps.title: Verify NATS installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the NATS pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> This command lists all pods in the NATS namespace.

steps.title: Create a NATS configuration
steps.description:
This step creates a custom NATS configuration to suit your needs.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

```

> This creates a NATS cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify NATS configuration
steps.description:
Ensure the NATS custom resource is ready and operational.

```bash

kubectl get nats -n nats

```

> This command checks the status of the NATS custom resource.

uninstall.title: Uninstall NATS
uninstall.description:
This step removes the NATS installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall nats --namespace nats

kubectl delete namespace nats

```

> Uninstall the NATS Helm release.
> Delete the NATS namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade NATS
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configuration. This step will upgrade NATS to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get nats -n nats -o yaml > nats-backup.yaml

helm upgrade nats nats/nats --namespace nats --version 2.11.0

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> Backup the current NATS configuration.
> Upgrade NATS to the specified version.
> Verify that the NATS pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get nats -n nats -o yaml > nats-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: NATS pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration in the NATS server settings.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the NATS pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n nats' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: NATS service is not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be properly exposed or the network policy might be blocking access.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service type is set to 'LoadBalancer' or 'NodePort' and check network policies.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to connect to NATS from client
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Client configuration may not match the server's authentication settings.

**Fix:**
Verify the client connection settings and ensure they align with the server's configuration.

troubleshooting.title: NATS server is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources allocated to the NATS pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource requests and limits in the NATS deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for JetStream, improved clustering capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.10, review the JetStream documentation for new features.

resolution.summary: You should see the NATS pods running with the command 'kubectl get pods -n nats' returning a status of 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
